name: MYCOPLASMA PNEUMONIA

demographics.Age: 5-20 years (most common, esp. in closed populations)
demographics.Histo: peribronchial mononuclear cell infiltrates (similar to viral lower respiratory infection)

dominant_finding: unilobar from hilum into lower lobe as earliest change (52%), bilobar (10%)
discriminating_features: unilobar from hilum into lower lobe as earliest change (52%), bilobar (10%)

general: unilobar from hilum into lower lobe as earliest change (52%), bilobar (10%)
patchy inhomogeneous unilateral (L > R) airspace consolidation in segmental lower lobe in

clinical.Prognosis: 20% with recurrent symptoms of pharyngitis + bronchitis ± infiltrations
